title: podman stop command doesn't stop systemd services in the container gracefully.
body:
<!--**
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
When I execute "podman stop" command for a container where several systemd services are running, the services don't stop gracefully. 

For example, while "postgresql-12" service is running in "postgresql" container, when I execute "systemd stop postgresql-12" inside the container, /var/log/messages includes the following log lines.
Aug  5 09:02:52 postgresql systemd[1]: Stopping PostgreSQL 12 database server...
Aug  5 09:02:52 postgresql systemd[1]: Stopped PostgreSQL 12 database server.

However when I execute "podman stop postgresql" command and then "podman start postgresql" and check /var/log/messages, the above log lines do not appear around the time when "podman stop postgresql" was executed. It looks like the service is killed suddenly. 

I understand that when we execute "podman stop [container]", SIGRTMIN+3 is thrown to the container. But it seems that the container does not handle the signal appropriately.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Execute "systemd stop [service]" inside a container and confirm "Stopping [service]" and "Stopped [service]" appear in /var/log/messages.

2. Execute "podman stop [container]" and then "podman start [container]".

3. Check the /var/log/messages inside the container.

**Describe the results you received:**

Log lines of "Stopping [service]" and "Stopped [service]" don't appear around the time when "podman stop [container]" was executed in the /var/log/messages .

**Describe the results you expected:**

Log lines of "Stopping [service]" and "Stopped [service]" should appear around the time when "podman stop [container]" was executed in the /var/log/messages .

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This issue always happens. 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.15.13
Built:        Wed Jul 21 13:07:51 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-3.2.3-1.el8.1.3.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

I confirmed this issue on CentOS 8.4.2105 on a VM using VirtualBox 6.1.26.

comments.body: Can you paste your systemd unit. `podman stop` has a default timeout of 10 seconds before it sends SIGKILL. You can increase the timeout with the `--time` option.

I'm assuming that systemd is running inside the container?

A `podman inspect` of the container in question would also help - want to verify that systemd mode is active and the correct stop signal has been set.

We should be sending a special signal (I honestly forget which - I think it's `SIGRTMIN+2` but not 100% sure) to systemd in the container, which causes it to shut down, which should include taking down everything it manages in the container in an orderly fashion. I'd expect to see log messages from systemd to this effect.

Yes, I make use of systemd which is running inside the container.

The systemd unit files I tested are default service unit files of PostgreSQL and OpenSSH services. Both services seem to be 
stopped suddenly without "systemdctl stop [service]" called for each service before they are killed. Therefore I assume that all the systemd services inside a container would have the same results. Though I added "--time 30" option for "podman stop" command, the results were the same.

Here is the output of "podman inspect" command. As for the special signal, I can see **"StopSignal": 37** in the output. So I think the stop signal is **SIGRTMIN+3**. But I don't know why systemd doesn't work well, when "podman stop [container]" is executed.

Could you bring the systemd guys in for this dicussion.
Concur - it doesn't sound like Podman is doing anything wrong here. I think we need to ask the systemd team what is going on.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

This should be brought up with systemd, we are sending the correct signal, if systemd is not handling it, then they have a bug or should tell us what is wrong.  When I run ubi8-init

I don't believe Podman has a bug, So closing.

Yes. This is not a bug of Podman.
I checked the shutdown messages by executing "**journalctl -ef**" on CentOS 8 inside a Podman container.
Then I was able to find out the following messages there.

> Sep 24 09:26:33 postgresql systemd[1]: Stopping PostgreSQL 12 database server...
:
> Sep 24 09:26:33 postgresql systemd[1]: Stopped PostgreSQL 12 database server.

Previously, I could not find these messages with other journalctl commands and /var/log/messages file. Because journald service does not seem to save any shutdown messages like above and old boot messages. I don't know why this happens, but I confirmed that Podman and Systemd are working correctly.
